hero.availability: Available for freelance projects — apps, AI products & automation
hero.name: Matt Roshay
hero.brandLine: From first idea to shipped product.
hero.descriptionBold: apps and AI products that actually ship
hero.descriptionEnd: — and the automations that keep them running.

whatIDo.title: What I do
whatIDo.subtext: Four ways I help teams go from AI curiosity to shipped results.

work.title: Selected work
work.subtext: A few recent client and product builds. From AI-powered apps to bilingual, SEO-first websites.

cta.title: Have a product to build — or a process that should run itself?
cta.description: Tell me what you want to build — or what's slow, manual, and held together by copy-paste. I'll tell you what's worth building, and what can just run itself. First call is free.

about.title: About me
about.paragraph1: I'm an AI Enablement specialist and full-stack developer based near Bordeaux, working with teams across France and beyond.
about.paragraph2: For 15 years I ran business development and client relationships inside international law firms — Herbert Smith Freehills, Freshfields, Covington — where my job was to turn what senior partners and clients actually needed into something a team could deliver. Then I learned to build it myself.
about.paragraph3: Today I do both. I translate fuzzy business asks into working automations, integrations, and products — built with APIs, n8n, Make, Dust, Claude, and modern web frameworks. The difference most freelance developers can't offer isn't the code; it's knowing which problem is worth solving in the first place, and how to talk to the people who own it.

stats.experience: 15+ years with senior stakeholders in international firms
stats.tooling: Certified: n8n L1 · Make L3 · Claude Code
stats.location: Near Bordeaux — working remotely, EN & FR

services.title: How I help
services.subtext: From first webhook to shipped product — pick where you are, and I'll meet you there.

aiWorkflow.title: AI Workflow Design & Implementation
aiWorkflow.hook: Automations that get used, not dashboards that collect dust.
aiWorkflow.body: I design and build AI-enabled workflows end to end — from the first webhook to the final action. APIs, n8n, Make, Dust, Claude. The kind of automation that quietly removes hours of manual work every week.

aiIntegration.title: AI Integration & Consulting
aiIntegration.hook: From "we should use AI" to shipped features.
aiIntegration.body: Adding AI to an existing product — OpenAI and Claude APIs, RAG patterns, prompt engineering, evaluation. I help teams move past the demo and put reliable AI features in front of real users.

webDev.title: Full-Stack Web & App Development
webDev.body: Modern web apps, PWAs, and internal tools — Next.js, React/TypeScript, Ruby on Rails, PostgreSQL, Supabase. Bilingual and SEO-ready when it matters. From first version to something you can put your name on.

discovery.title: Discovery & Scoping
discovery.hook: Figuring out what to build is the hard part.
discovery.body: Often harder than building it. I map your current workflows, surface the real inefficiencies, and scope realistic AI and automation projects with clear success criteria — before a line of code is written. Fifteen years translating fuzzy asks into concrete deliverables inside complex organisations.

skills.title: Skills & Technologies
skills.description: A carefully curated toolkit that enables me to build exceptional digital experiences from concept to deployment.

philosophy.title: My Development Philosophy
philosophy.description: I believe in writing clean, maintainable code that scales. Every project is an opportunity to learn something new and push the boundaries of what's possible on the web. I stay current with emerging technologies while maintaining a strong foundation in proven solutions. My goal is to deliver not just functional software, but delightful user experiences that drive real value.

projects.title: Selected work
projects.description: Client engagements and products — AI apps, automations, and websites built to ship and be used.

sarahPsy.title: Sarah Cousin Roshay — Bilingual Therapy Practice
sarahPsy.label: Client project · Live
sarahPsy.description: A bilingual (EN/FR) website for a CBT therapist serving expats, professionals, and parents. Built for the two things that win clients online: being found and being trusted. Per-page SEO, clean multilingual routing, testimonials, and one-tap discovery-call booking. Design, build, SEO, and copy.
sarahPsy.imageAlt: sarah-psy.com homepage: bilingual therapy practice website with discovery-call booking

bitconsulting.title: B-itconsulting
bitconsulting.label: Client project · Live
bitconsulting.description: Portfolio and blog CMS for an IT infrastructure consultant, with automated LinkedIn posting, content management, and a responsive build. Content published once flows straight to LinkedIn — a small automation that saves a manual step every post.
bitconsulting.imageAlt: B-itconsulting portfolio and blog CMS homepage

matchmeal.title: MatchMeal
matchmeal.label: Founder · Product
matchmeal.description: An AI recipe app I founded: photograph your ingredients, and it identifies them and suggests personalised meals via the OpenAI API. Image uploads, favourites, and an adaptive UI. Built and shipped end to end.
matchmeal.imageAlt: MatchMeal AI recipe app showing ingredient recognition and meal suggestions

flatrent.title: FlatRent
flatrent.label: Le Wagon capstone
flatrent.description: A full-stack booking platform inspired by Airbnb — my Le Wagon capstone. Listings, authentication, search filters, reservations, maps, and payments. Where I proved I could ship a complete product.
flatrent.imageAlt: FlatRent booking platform property listings page

contact.title: Let's talk about your project
contact.description: The best first step is a quick call. Tell me what's slow, manual, or on your roadmap — I'll tell you honestly whether I'm the right fit and what it would take.

calendly.title: Book a free discovery call
calendly.subtitle: 30 minutes, no obligation. We'll talk through your problem and whether it's worth building.
calendly.fallback: Prefer to write first? Use the form below.

form.title: Or send a message
form.name: Name
form.subject: Subject
form.messagePlaceholder: Tell me about your project, timeline, and what success looks like.
form.consentNotice: The details you send are used only to reply to your message and are never shared for marketing — see the

info.title: Get in touch
info.description: Whether you're scoping a first AI feature or modernising an existing product, I'm happy to help you think it through.
info.quickResponseDescription: I usually reply within 24 hours. For anything urgent, call directly.

footer.description: AI Enablement & Product Builder. I build full-stack apps and AI products — and the workflows and integrations that run around them.

sections.heading: Site publisher
paragraphs: In accordance with Article 6 of French law n° 2004-575 of 21 June 2004 on confidence in the digital economy (LCEN), this website (mattroshay.com) is published by:
items: Matthieu Roshay, sole proprietor (entrepreneur individuel) under French law
Registered office: 5 allée de Terre Vieille, 33160 Saint-Médard-en-Jalles, France
Registered in the French National Business Register (RNE) since 12 August 2025
VAT not applicable — Article 293 B of the French General Tax Code (franchise en base)

sections.heading: Contact
paragraphs: You can reach the publisher through the contact form on this site, or by phone at +33 6 74 94 12 49.

sections.heading: Hosting
paragraphs: This site is hosted by Heroku, a service of Salesforce, Inc., 415 Mission Street, Suite 300, San Francisco, CA 94105, United States — heroku.com.

sections.heading: Intellectual property
paragraphs: Unless stated otherwise, all content on this site (text, images, logos, code and layout) is the property of Matthieu Roshay and is protected by French and international intellectual-property law. Any reproduction, distribution or modification, in whole or in part, without prior written permission is prohibited.
Third-party trademarks and logos mentioned on this site (clients, tools, platforms) remain the property of their respective owners.

sections.heading: Liability
paragraphs: The information published on this site is provided for general information purposes and may be updated at any time. Despite the care taken in its preparation, it may contain inaccuracies or omissions; the publisher cannot be held liable for any direct or indirect damage arising from the use of this site.
This site contains links to external websites. The publisher has no control over their content and accepts no responsibility for them.

sections.heading: Applicable law
paragraphs: This site is governed by French law. Any dispute relating to its use falls under the jurisdiction of the French courts.

sections.heading: Who is responsible for your data
paragraphs: Matthieu Roshay, sole proprietor (entrepreneur individuel), 5 allée de Terre Vieille, 33160 Saint-Médard-en-Jalles, France — SIREN 990 225 419 — is the data controller for personal data collected on mattroshay.com. Full identification details are available on the Legal Notice page.

sections.heading: What data is collected, and why
items: Contact form — name, email address, subject and message. Purpose: answering your enquiry. Legal basis: steps taken at your request prior to entering into a contract, or legitimate interest in responding (Art. 6(1)(b) and 6(1)(f) GDPR).
Call booking — if you book a call, your name, email and chosen time slot are collected directly by Calendly on its own site, under Calendly's privacy policy.

Audience measurement — with your consent, Google Tag Manager and Google Analytics collect browsing data (pages visited, approximate location, device type) to understand how the site is used. Legal basis: consent (Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR).
paragraphs: No personal data is sold or rented to third parties, and the site collects nothing beyond what is listed above.

sections.heading: Service providers and data transfers
paragraphs: The following processors handle data on the publisher's behalf:
items: Google Ireland Ltd / Google LLC — audience measurement, only if you consent
Salesforce, Inc. — Heroku (United States) — site hosting

sections.heading: Transfers outside the European Union
paragraphs: Some of these providers are located in the United States. Where data is transferred outside the EU, the transfer relies on safeguards recognised by the GDPR, such as the EU–U.S. Data Privacy Framework or the European Commission's Standard Contractual Clauses.

sections.heading: How long data is kept
paragraphs: Contact-form messages are kept for the time needed to handle the exchange, and at most 12 months after our last contact — unless we start working together, in which case legal contractual and accounting retention periods apply. Audience-measurement data is retained for at most 14 months.

sections.heading: Cookies and trackers
paragraphs: Cookies and similar trackers used for audience measurement are only set after you give consent via the cookie banner, and you can withdraw that consent at any time without affecting your browsing.
Strictly necessary local storage — such as remembering your language preference (EN/FR) — does not require consent.

sections.heading: Your rights
paragraphs: Under the GDPR and the French Data Protection Act (Loi Informatique et Libertés), you have the right to access, rectify and erase your personal data, to restrict or object to its processing, and to receive it in a portable format.
To exercise these rights, contact me via the contact form or by phone at +33 6 74 94 12 49. If you believe your rights have not been respected, you may lodge a complaint with the CNIL (cnil.fr).

consent.message: This site uses cookies for audience measurement (Google Analytics). They are only set if you accept — declining changes nothing about your browsing.
